title: Merck — Transplant — briefing Q3-2025
html:
What Changed in Merck Transplant — Q3 2025

Generated: 2026-04-26 (retrospective) Quarter covered: July - September 2025 Data sources: Merck Q3 2025 earnings (October 2025), ZoomRx Message Recall Study Wave 1 fielding, FDA actions, clinical literature Reading time: ~8 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. Prevymis H1 2025 revenue: $436M (+20.4% YoY) — kidney transplant uptake continues to accelerate, now the primary growth driver beyond the mature HSCT base. Merck attributes growth to "higher demand in the U.S." from new indications.
  2. ZoomRx Message Recall Study Wave 1 fielding completed (May 30 - Aug 4, 2025) — 102 KT HCPs and 93 HSCT HCPs surveyed. This is the richest promotional effectiveness dataset for Prevymis in both indications.
  3. KT is being prioritized over HSCT in multi-indication rep details — 75% of IDs/pharmacists at dual-indication institutions receive both KT + HSCT discussions, with KT discussed first and given more time. This confirms Merck's strategic emphasis on the newer, larger market.
  4. Prevymis leads SOV in both personal and non-personal promotions — outpacing Livtencity and CytoGam across rep reach, frequency, and NPP exposure (>90% of HCPs reached by NPP, >40% SOV).
  5. European Commission approved Prevymis for KT and extended 200-day HSCT dosing — international expansion opens significant additional revenue runway beyond the US market.

2. Data Events That Matter

ZoomRx Message Recall Study — Wave 1 Complete

  • What happened: Fielding for the combined KT + HSCT Message Recall Study completed August 4, 2025. Sample: KT n=102, HSCT n=93. HCPs included transplant nephrologists, infectious disease physicians, hematologist-oncologists, NP/PAs, and pharmacists.
  • What it means: This provides the first comprehensive promotional effectiveness benchmark for Prevymis across both indications simultaneously. The study measures share of voice, message recall and effectiveness, rep performance, NPP performance, and post-interaction behavior change.
  • What Vinoth should say: "This study gives Merck their first dual-indication promotional scorecard. The biggest insight will be whether the KT promotional engine is performing at the same level as the mature HSCT engine, and where the gaps are."

EC Approval — KT and Extended HSCT Dosing in Europe

  • What happened: The European Commission approved Prevymis for prevention of CMV disease in high-risk adult kidney transplant recipients and extended 200-day dosing in adult HSCT recipients at high risk for late CMV infection.
  • What it means: Europe is a significant incremental market. The approval enables revenue diversification beyond the US and extends the addressable HSCT population (high-risk patients who need prophylaxis beyond Day 100).
  • What Vinoth should say: "The EC approval is important because it doubles the geographic runway for the KT indication. International revenue will increasingly contribute to Prevymis's trajectory toward $1B."

Prevymis Revenue Momentum

  • What happened: H1 2025 Prevymis sales reached $436M, growing 20.4% YoY.
  • What it means: The KT indication launch is translating into real revenue growth. At this trajectory, FY 2025 could approach $900M-$1B.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Prevymis is one of Merck's fastest-growing hospital products. The 20% growth rate on a $900M+ base means this franchise is contributing meaningful revenue at a time when Merck needs non-Keytruda growth stories."

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric H1 2025 (actual) Commentary
Prevymis Revenue (H1) $436M +20.4% YoY; KT driving growth
KT Message Recall Study Fielding complete Report in development
EC Approval Granted KT + extended HSCT 200-day
SOV Leadership Maintained Prevymis leads in both PP and NPP

4. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q3 2025

Company Brand Q3 2025 Revenue Key Signal
Takeda Livtencity (maribavir) ~$30M Niche CMV treatment for refractory/resistant cases; no prophylaxis threat
Prevymis ~$220M (H1: $436M) 20.4% YoY growth; KT driving acceleration

Takeda — Livtencity (~$30M): Livtencity remains confined to the CMV treatment-failure niche — patients where Prevymis prophylaxis or other antivirals have failed. Takeda's Q3 commentary emphasized the refractory/resistant positioning, not prophylaxis expansion. At ~$30M/quarter, Livtencity is roughly 1/7th of Prevymis's revenue and growing slowly. The relationship between the two products is complementary (prophylaxis vs. treatment failure), not competitive. The real competitive dynamic for Prevymis remains generic valganciclovir on cost and formulary expansion in kidney transplant. This is a low-competition space — Prevymis's growth is driven by indication expansion and adoption, not by taking share from competitors.

What Merck management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. Prevymis was mentioned briefly in "established brands" — not positioned as a named growth driver despite 20%+ YoY growth. This reflects Merck's Keytruda-centric earnings narrative, where non-oncology assets receive minimal airtime.
  2. "Higher demand in the U.S." was attributed to new indications — management confirmed that kidney transplant adoption is the primary growth vector, though specific KT revenue was not broken out.
  3. The European Commission approval for KT and extended 200-day HSCT dosing was mentioned in the regulatory update section but not emphasized in management's prepared remarks — further evidence that Prevymis, despite strong growth, is not yet in the CEO/CFO narrative.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. No analyst questions were asked about Prevymis on the Q3 2025 call. At $220M quarterly ($900M annual run rate), the product is below the materiality threshold for most sell-side analysts covering Merck.
  2. This invisibility is both a risk and an opportunity: a risk because Merck may underinvest in commercial support for a product the Street doesn't track; an opportunity because strong results can create a positive surprise narrative when Prevymis eventually crosses $1B.

5. ZoomRx Engagement Health

Study Status Next Milestone
Message Recall Study (KT + HSCT) Wave 1 Fielding complete Report delivery expected Q4 2025
KT ATU Wave 2 Complete (2024) Wave 3 timing TBD

6. What's Coming Next Quarter

  • Message Recall Study full report delivery — expect actionable findings on message gaps and rep performance
  • Merck Q3 2025 earnings — will confirm whether H1 momentum sustained into Q3; watch for Prevymis-specific revenue disclosure
  • International KT launch traction — first evidence of EU KT commercial uptake

Prevymis H1 2025 revenue: $436M (+20.4% YoY) — kidney transplant uptake continues to accelerate, now the primary growth driver beyond the mature HSCT base. Merck attributes growth to "higher demand in the U.S." from new indications.

KT is being prioritized over HSCT in multi-indication rep details — 75% of IDs/pharmacists at dual-indication institutions receive both KT + HSCT discussions, with KT discussed first and given more time. This confirms Merck's strategic emphasis on the newer, larger market.

European Commission approved Prevymis for KT and extended 200-day HSCT dosing — international expansion opens significant additional revenue runway beyond the US market.
